6-12: Here, the rule followed is: the sum of the digits is calculated. Hence,
I: the number with lowest sum of its digits gets interchanged. Hence,
the number. (If the first number has already the lowest sum of digits, then
the number with second lowest sum of its digits gets interchanged with
second number.) The process continues until the numbers arranged in
according order on the basis of their sum of digits.
7.1: Input: 44, 653, 325, 688, 401, 231, 857
Step I: 8 (4+4) (10) (22) (11) (6) (20)
Step I: 6 14 10 22 11 13 20
Step II: 6 10 11 13 14 20 22
Step III: 6 10 11 22 13 14 20
Step IV: 6 10 11 13 14 20 22
Step V: 6 10 11 13 14 20 22
Hence, 231, 325, 401, 544, 653, 857, 688
[Note: It is easy to process with the help of sum of their digits and also when required.]

10.4: In these types, where arrangement or ordering is involved, previous steps can't be determined. [See previous chapter.]

13-17: The words are arranged according to the number of letters they have one at a time. The word with the maximum number of letters is pushed two words have the same number of letters, we go for alphabetic arrangement.

Step I: car he as he may me
Step II: car may he as he may he
Step III: car may he as in he me

25: the rule followed is: The first and the last numbers are interchanged in each step in and so are the second and the second-last in second step and so on in each step, until the series is arranged in reverse order.
Now, the sum of digits is calculated and square of that sum is substituted in the next step.
The next step is arrived at by deducting 2, 4, 6, 8 ... (depends on no. of items in the input) from the number in the previous step respectively. Hereafter, the same process is repeated.
6.1: Input: 87, 58, 49, 92, 21
Step I: 27, 58, 49, 92, 21, 87
Step II: 27, 58, 49, 21, 92, 58, 87
Step III: 27, 21, 92, 49, 58, 87
Step IV: 27, only for arrangement so when we start from input, in step III, the nos. get arranged in reverse order. Now, step IV will be the lowest.
Squares of sum of the digits of each no.
Hence, when we start from step IV towards input, because of the immediate possibilities of sum of digits of nos., we can't determine the previous step.
21 → 3² = 4; Process never ends.    22.1
23.5: 29 ⇒ (2 + 9 =) 11 ⇒ 121
121 ÷ 8 = 15.125 [as the nos. are reversed in step IV];
62 ⇒ 8² = 64; 64 ÷ 12 = 52
24.3: Here, the rule followed is: The numbers are getting arranged in alternate series; one in descending order and the other in ascending order.
Step I:  largest of the given numbers comes to the place of first number and the rest shift rightward.
Step II:  The smallest of the given numbers comes to the place of second number and the rest shift rightward.
Step III: The second largest of the given numbers comes to the place of third number and the rest shift rightward.
Step IV: The second smallest of the given numbers comes to the place of fourth number and the rest shift rightward and so on until the alternate series is formed.
24.1: Step III: 631,29,520,474,48,312,502,36,68
Step IV: 631,29,520,36,474,48,312,502,68
